channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Follow NPR's annotated fact check of President Trump's address to Congress
item.description: As President Donald Trump addresses a joint session of Congress tonight, reporters from across NPR's newsroom will fact check and offer context as the speech unfolds.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: Senate confirms former Trump defense attorney Todd Blanche as deputy attorney general
item.description: The Republican-led Senate has voted to confirm Todd Blanche as deputy attorney general, placing President Donald Trump’s former criminal defense attorney in a key Justice Department post at a time of turmoil in the agency.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Resolution punishing Al Green clears Dem blockade, advances to House-wide vote after Trump speech
item.description: A resolution to formally punish Rep. Al Green for protesting President Donald Trump's speech is possibly getting a House vote this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein files latest: AG Pam Bondi says more evidence to be released
item.description: Pam Bondi said, "We will redact it, of course, to protect grand jury information and confidential witnesses.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: Karoline Leavitt explains US efforts to negotiate with Hamas to free American hostages
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt explained the United States' negotiations with Hamas aimed at securing the release of American hostages during a Wednesday briefing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Veterans are speaking out on the Trump administration's plans to cut the VA's budget
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Stephen Watson served in the Marines for 22 years and receives care through the Department of Veterans Affairs for a traumatic brain injury. He supports President Donald Trump and adviser Elon Musk's cost-cutting program — even if it affects the VA.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dueling Al Green censure efforts stir frustrations within GOP as Republicans demand consequences
item.description: There are three different resolutions circulating to punish Rep. Al Green for repeatedly protesting President Donald Trump's joint address to Congress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 6 times Trump bashed Biden in address to Congress
item.description: While addressing Congress, President Donald Trump slammed former President Joe Biden for his record on immigration, the economy, energy and more.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republican introduces censure resolution against Al Green
item.description: The House will vote this week on a resolution from Rep. Dan Newhouse (R-Wash.) to censure Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) for his outburst at President Trump’s speech to a joint session of Congress on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump admin plans to cut 80,000 employees from Veterans Affairs: internal memo
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs is planning an “aggressive” reorganization that includes cutting 80,000 jobs from the sprawling agency that provides health care for retired military members, according to an internal memo obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia Democrat Lucy McBath says she's exploring a 2026 run for governor
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia U.S. Rep. Lucy McBath filed paperwork on Wednesday to raise money for a run for governor in 2026, becoming the first Democrat to take that step. McBath described her effort as an exploratory committee, although that could quickly become a full-blown campaign.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Divided SCOTUS rejects Trump administration's push to rebuke judge over foreign aid freeze
item.description: By a 5-4 vote Wednesday, the Supreme Court told U.S. District Judge Amir Ali to clarify his earlier order that required the Republican administration to release nearly $2 billion in aid for work that had already been done.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota farmers fret about ‘chaos and uncertainty’ following latest Trump tariffs
item.description: Minnesota producers say they’re worried about the impact that tariffs on Canada, China and Mexico coupled with retaliatory tariffs could have on the agriculture industry.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Democrats behavior presented party as 'hateful': Bill O'Reilly
item.description: Bill O'Reilly says the behavior from the Democrats was 'hateful' as they did not acknowledge things presented by Trump.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Spanish-language rebuttal to Trump’s joint address focuses on immigration
item.description: New York Rep. Adriano Espaillat delivered the Democrats’ Spanish-language rebuttal to President Trump’s joint address, focusing on the president’s immigration policy.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Trump passes Clinton, gives longest modern address to joint session of Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump gave the longest address to a joint session of Congress in modern history on Tuesday night, surpassing former President Bill Clinton’s previous mark.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 6 takeaways from Trump’s pointedly partisan address to Congress
item.description: After a firehose of a first six weeks back in the White House, President Trump delivered a boastful and partisan address to a joint session of Congress Tuesday night. Here are six takeaways from the speech.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Democrats disrupt, protest and wear pained expressions for Trump’s speech
item.description: House Democrats greeted President Trump’s return to Congress on Tuesday with a spirited series of disruptive protests — and a number of painful looks as they suffered through an address in which the returning president repeatedly cajoled and mocked them.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Fired federal workers are among the guests invited to Trump's speech to Congress
item.description: The Trumps and lawmakers from across the aisle have invited guests to Tuesday's joint address, representing some of the administration's top priorities. Here's a look at some of the names on the list.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Dan Goldman looks to be 'the man in the arena' against Trump
item.description: Rep. Dan Goldman (D-N.Y.) wasn’t planning to run for Congress after his stint as the lead counsel on President Trump’s first impeachment.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: In polarized times, these Minnesota legislators want to restore civility at the state Capitol
item.description: This year’s legislative session started with a lot of vitriol between DFLers and Republicans. But a bipartisan group of legislators is trying to get both sides to be more collaborative and civil.
item.dc:creator: Catharine Richert, MPR News

item.title: Jenkins won’t seek fourth term on Minneapolis City Council
item.description: Before she became the first Black openly trans woman to be elected to office in the United States in 2017, Andrea Jenkins served as a policy aide in city hall.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Billions in federal funding at stake for sanctuary cities
item.description: The mayors of Denver, Chicago, Boston and New York will appear before a GOP-led committee to discuss sanctuary laws.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens increased tariffs if Canada retaliates
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday threatened to add additional taxes on Canada if the U.S.'s northern neighbor Canada retaliates with its own after Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau slammed Trump’s newly implemented tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: A crucial, vacant Minnesota House seat will finally be filled next week
item.description: The March 11 special election will determine whether Republicans secure enough seats to pass bills in the Minnesota House or if they’ll fall back into a tie with the DFL.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: 5 things to watch as Trump delivers joint address to Congress
item.description: President Trump will head to the Capitol on Tuesday for the first joint address of his second term emboldened by a month of mass federal agency slashing, shocking foreign policy moves and a dizzying array of executive actions that have disrupted Washington and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump trade war intensifies as tariffs go into place
item.description: Americans awoke on Tuesday morning to an intensifying trade war, as President Donald Trump’s newest tariffs on Canadian, Mexican and Chinese imports were imposed and those countries quickly moved to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Senate negotiators look at dueling options to avert government shutdown
item.description: Top appropriators are still in search of a deal to fund the government with 10 days until a shutdown deadline, as talk in the Senate turn to two possibilities for stopgap spending bills.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration ramps up attacks as Zelenskyy thanks US
item.description: There is a growing chorus of Republicans calling for new Ukrainian leadership following Friday's Oval Office meeting.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, Fox 5

item.title: Judge extends block on Trump gender-affirming care federal funding ban
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge Lauren King ruled that President Trump’s executive orders violate the Constitution’s Fifth Amendment, which grants equal protection under the law.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Zelenskyy's White House meeting with Trump, Vance turns to shouting match
item.description: A White House meeting that was intended to kick off negotiations over a deal that would offer the United States access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals unraveled Friday afternoon as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy clashed with President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance in the Oval Of
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, ZEKE MILLER and JUSTIN SPIKE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Democrats push for more ballot drop boxes to avoid election delays
item.description: Nevada Democrats unveiled a proposal Thursday to increase the number of ballot drop boxes in the days before an election.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elon Musk urges retired air traffic controllers to come back
item.description: Earlier this month, President Trump’s administration began firing hundreds of workers at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), including personnel brought on for the FAA radar as well as landing and navigational aid maintenance.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5
